preview_text:
The model backs Belgium to win at 38%, but marks this as a relatively modest favorite. Iran's win probability of 30% sits only 8 percentage points below Belgium's, positioning the underdog as a genuine threat rather than a long shot.

The 31% draw probability signals a competitive match with narrow margins expected across outcomes. The win probabilities cluster tightly—no outcome commands dominant likelihood—suggesting either team could reasonably advance or the fixture could remain level after 90 minutes. Belgium holds an edge but the model describes a genuinely uncertain contest.

A substantial gap separates model and market. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both assign Belgium roughly 67% to win, some 29 percentage points higher than the model's estimate. The market correspondingly prices Iran at around 12%, half the model's 30%. This divergence is material and deliberate; the dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is correct, only that consensus opinions diverge meaningfully from the statistical forecast.
